headline: The S&P 500 Just Had Its Best Month Since 2020. Don’t Let ‘Sell in May’ Spook You.
summary: There was a lot that could have made the stock market fall.  Investors shrugged off questions about when—or even if—the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates again this year after the central bank held rates steady, and it ignored the fact that Jerome Powell plans to stay on the Fed’s Board of Governors even as Kevin Warsh prepares to become chair. Inflation came in hot as oil prices continued to rise, and earnings from  Microsoft  and  Meta Platforms  revealed sizable capital spending increases—and resulted in falling stocks.  Eli Lilly  earnings offered evidence that weight-loss drugs were only getting bigger;  Coca-Cola’s  beat showed that not all soft-drink makers are suffering from an Ozempic overhang;  Alphabet’s  capital spending paid off with big profits; and  Caterpillar’s  numbers demonstrated that there’s more to its business than mining and machinery.

content.title: The S&P 500 Just Had Its Best Month Since 2020. Don’t Let ‘Sell in May’ Spook You.
content.summary: There was a lot that could have made the stock market fall.  Investors shrugged off questions about when—or even if—the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates again this year after the central bank held rates steady, and it ignored the fact that Jerome Powell plans to stay on the Fed’s Board of Governors even as Kevin Warsh prepares to become chair. Inflation came in hot as oil prices continued to rise, and earnings from  Microsoft  and  Meta Platforms  revealed sizable capital spending increases—and resulted in falling stocks.  Eli Lilly  earnings offered evidence that weight-loss drugs were only getting bigger;  Coca-Cola’s  beat showed that not all soft-drink makers are suffering from an Ozempic overhang;  Alphabet’s  capital spending paid off with big profits; and  Caterpillar’s  numbers demonstrated that there’s more to its business than mining and machinery.

headline: Earnings: Google is biggest AI winner, OpenAI could be a 'surprise' loser
summary: Five of the tech stocks making up the Magnificent Seven reported earnings this week — Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT). Niles Investment Management founder and portfolio manager Dan Niles comes on Market Domination to make his case as to why Google's parent company may be Big Tech's biggest winner in AI this quarter. He goes on to share his perspective on Microsoft (MSFT) and OpenAI's (OPAI.PVT) standings in the AI race. Nvidia (NVDA) will close out the Mag 7's earnings season when it releases its first quarter 2027 results on Wednesday, May 20.

content.title: Earnings: Google is biggest AI winner, OpenAI could be a 'surprise' loser
content.description:
Five of the tech stocks making up the Magnificent Seven reported earnings this week — Alphabet (GOOG, GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), Apple (AAPL), Meta Platforms (META), and Microsoft (MSFT).

Niles Investment Management founder and portfolio manager Dan Niles comes on Market Domination to make his case as to why Google's parent company may be Big Tech's biggest winner in AI this quarter. He goes on to share his perspective on Microsoft (MSFT) and OpenAI's (OPAI.PVT) standings in the AI race.

Nvidia (NVDA) will close out the Mag 7's earnings season when it releases its first quarter 2027 results on Wednesday, May 20.

He goes on to share his perspective on Microsoft (MSFT) and OpenAI's (OPAI.PVT) standings in the AI race. Nvidia (NVDA) will close out the Mag 7's earnings season when it releases its first quarter 2027 results on Wednesday, May 20.
